The '''T'surr''' were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] indigenous to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[T'surr]]. T'surr were rarely seen in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. The species was among the galaxy's most dangerous predators, although their relative scarcity made them less threatening. One of the most famous T'surr was the notorious [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] and [[Slavery/Legends|slave]] trader [[Krayn]]. Although their level of technology did not meet the galactic standard, T'surr capably used technology scavenged from the ships of their victims.

==Biology and appearance==
[[File:SWKnightErrant1b.jpg|thumb|left|180px|A T'surr battles Jedi [[Kerra Holt]]]]
The T'surr were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Color/Legends|blue]] skinned<ref name="PTTC">[[Star Wars: Jedi Quest (comic series)|''Star Wars: Jedi Quest'' comic adaptation]]</ref> and blue [[Blood/Legends|blooded]]<ref name="TSL">[[Dark Times 4|''Dark Times'' 4]]</ref> predators. Their appearance followed that of most humanoids, with members of the species possessing a bipedal stance with two [[Limb/Legends|legs]] and four arms. Two of their arms ended in<ref name="PTTC" /> meaty hands<ref name="PTT">''[[Jedi Quest: Path to Truth]]''</ref> with either five<ref name="PTTC" /> or three fingers,<ref name="UAAP">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20030327tsurr|text=T'surr: ''Ultimate Alien Anthology'' Preview #4}} {{1stID|T'surr}}</ref> while the second pair was far smaller than the first and protruded from their upper torso<ref name="Star Wars: Dark Times">''[[Star Wars: Dark Times]]''</ref> or arms and ended in a crab like claw.<ref name="PTTC" /> The species displayed sexual dimorphism between its two sexes, with males on average standing between 2.3 and 2.6 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] tall, while females stood at only around 2 meters. Males were also physically stronger than females, being solidly built,<ref name="UAAP" /> with some males being able to spin a [[Vibro-ax/Legends|vibro-ax]] in one hand like a toy.<ref name="PTT" />

A T'surr's head included a mouth full of razor sharp [[Tooth/Legends|teeth]], a nose, and two sets of gleaming red pupiless [[Eye/Legends|eyes]],<ref name="UAAP" /> with either one set situated above the other<ref name="PTTC" /> or all four eyes in a horizontal line above the nose.<ref name="UAAP" /> Around the eyes and mouth a T'surr's face was heavily ridged, with the creases slanting out toward the fan like sides of the head,<ref name="PTTC" /> which extended into a large number of horns in males.<ref name="UAAP" /> T'surr were capable of growing hair on their heads, including beards,<ref name="PTTC" /> which could be black in color. A T'surr's face was capable of producing expressions of nervousness, amusement, sadness and astonishment amongst others, and when a T'surr was enraged its neck would flush red.<ref name="PTT" />

==Society and culture==
T'surr originated from the [[Desert/Legends|desert]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[T'surr|the same name]]<ref name="UAAP" /> in the [[T'surr system/Legends|T'surr system]] of the [[Nuon e Safyd sector/Legends|Nuon e Safyd sector]], a [[Sector/Legends|sector]] in the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region]] of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].<ref name="EAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> Their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] was cold and dark due to its distant [[Star/Legends|star]] and constantly windblown, with other species finding the high gravity there daunting and oppressive. These conditions created a race of survivors who had little empathy for other beings, making practices like [[Slavery/Legends|slavery]] common and accepted amongst the T'surr. Within T'surr society wealth was judged based upon how many slaves an individual owned as well as their quality, with a T'surr with no slaves was seen as worthless and was looked down upon by others.

Male T'surr were violent and cruel in nature, looking down upon weaker species and treating them as livestock to be controlled. A T'surr would use his strength and cunning to control other males of his own species as well as any member of [[Alien/Legends|other]] species, bullying them to get his way. This was not the case when it came to male treatment of T'surr females however, as the female T'surr controlled the species homeworld. Males would anxiously leave the female dominated T'surr, also allowing mothers to raise any children. Upon leaving their home, T'surr were often drawn to violent, predatory, professions, joining forces with powerful bands of [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]], [[Privateer/Legends|privateers]], or [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]]. They liked to keep trophies as reminders of their conquests, often wearing these stolen trinkets on their belts. Their species spoke a [[Language/Legends|language]] called [[T'surrese]]. T'surr names included Datynask, Dyrago, Feyrr, Jeneyd, Reythan, Shyntak and Treykon.<ref name="UAAP" />

==History==
[[File:TsurrGreenbark-DarkTimes3.jpg|thumb|250px|T'surr apprehend [[Bomo Greenbark]] on [[Orvax IV]] in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]].]]
Though they were long present in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], the T'surr were never represented in any galactic government. The [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] never allowed them representation in the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]] because they were widely regarded as nothing more than dangerous, marauding killers. However, some governments, like [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Daiman]]'s [[Daimanate]], happily used members of the species as [[Sith trooper (Sith Empire)|Sith troopers]]<ref name="Errant">[[Knight Errant 1|''Knight Errant'' 1]]</ref>. They were also preferred guards used by the slave traders on Orvax IV.<ref name="TSL" />

During the last [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the Republic, a notorious T'surr named Krayn worked as a pirate and slaver. His organization was infiltrated by a [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] named [[Siri Tachi/Legends|Siri Tachi]], and during her undercover operation, [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] and his [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]], [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]], also fell into Krayn's custody. Though he was a successful criminal and a powerful fighter, Krayn was no match for Skywalker, who had once been a slave himself, and gave in to his anger when faced with Krayn's brutality. The young [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] attacked and [[Death/Legends|killed]] Krayn with his [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]].

The [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] continued the policy of excluding the T'surr from galactic [[Politics/Legends|politics]].

==T'surr in the galaxy==
Amongst the most infamous of the T'surr was [[Krayn]], a [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] and [[Slavery/Legends|slaver]] who operated during the final [[Standard decade/Legends|decades]] of the Republic. In the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[44 BBY/Legends|44 BBY]] he [[Mos Eisley slave raid|raided]] the [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Mos Eisley/Legends|Mos Eisley]], capturing a number of slaves from the [[Desert/Legends|desert]] [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]]. [[28 BBY/Legends|Sixteen years later]], Krayn was still active and had come to control much of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Nar Shaddaa/Legends|Nar Shaddaa]], partly through its ruler [[Aga Culpa]]. Due to this power, he was able to make a deal with members of the [[Colicoid/Legends|Colicoid]] species who hoped to take over the galactic [[Spice/Legends|spice]] trade. Krayn would be the sole provider of slaves for the Colicoids and in return he agreed to stop attacking Colicoid ships, although in secret he arranged for an attack on a [[Colicoid cargo ship]] being piloted by the Colicoid [[Anf Dec]]. Krayn was present for the attack, but it was foiled by two [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] and his [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]], who boarded Karyn's ship and disabled its weapons systems, forcing the pirate to retreat.

Krayn and his crew then traveled to Nar Shaddaa to meet with the Coliocoids to discuss their arrangement. While there he submitted to a review of his operations by an agent selected by the Colicoids, named "[[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Bakleeda]]," and discovered that one of his crew, "Zora," was in fact a Jedi, whose real name was [[Siri Tachi/Legends|Siri Tachi]]. Tachi, who, in truth, was working undercover for the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]], was imprisoned as a result for her treachery, but the Jedi agent managed to escape with the help of Skywalker and Kenobi, who'd assumed the identities of a slave and "Bakleeda," respectively, and who'd turned Krayn's allies against him. After Culpa and the Colicoids abandoned Krayn, and his slaves had [[Nar Shaddaa slave revolt|revolted]] against him, Skywalker sought the slaver out and killed him by impaling him with his lightsaber.<ref name="PTT" />

==Behind the scenes==
The T'surr species first appeared in ''[[Jedi Quest: Path to Truth]]'', a novel written by [[Jude Watson]] in the [[Star Wars: Jedi Quest|''Jedi Quest'']] series and the [[Star Wars: Jedi Quest (comic series)|series]] of [[Star Wars comic books|comic books]] based on the book and released at the same time. The comics, which were written by [[Ryder Windham]] and illustrated by [[Pop Mhan]], had a number of significant differences in terms of the appearance of the T'surr character Krayn, whose species wasn't named in the book or comic. In the comics there is a notable difference between Krayn's appearance in the first issue compared to those that follow. In the first issue he lacks a nose and has a number of spines running down the back of his neck, however in the three later issues he has a clear nose and no spines. This article assumes the three later articles are correct as they match later illustrations of T'surr more closely.

The species next appeared in [[2003]] as part of a series of previews for the soon-to-be-released sourcebook ''[[Ultimate Alien Anthology]]'' for the [[Wizards of the Coast]] ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]''. The article was the first to identify Krayn's species as being T'surr and provided roleplaying rules for the species. It was written by XXX with an illustration by [[Kalman Andrasofszky]]. The sourcebook contained all of the same content concerning the T'surr and was released later that same year. In [[2006]] a T'surr appeared in ''[[Evasive Action: Prey]]'', a comic strip written [[Paul Ens]] and illustrated by [[Tom Hodges|Thomas Hodges]], which was released on the [[Hyperspace: The Official Star Wars Fan Club|Hyperspace]] section of the [[StarWars.com]] website. The comic series ''[[Star Wars: Dark Times]]'' also included a number of T'surr characters in the [[Star Wars: Dark Times: The Path to Nowhere|''The Path to Nowhere'']] arc released in [[2006]] and the [[Star Wars: Dark Times: Parallels|''Parallels'']] arc released in [[2009]].
